evals.prompt: I want to add MatrixScan AR to my Kotlin Multiplatform app. Here's my empty shared screen model: EmptyScannerScreenModel.kt. I need to scan EAN-13 and QR barcodes and show a rectangle highlight on each one.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md and writes a complete BarcodeAr integration into EmptyScannerScreenModel.kt: DataCaptureContext.initialize with a license key placeholder, BarcodeArSettings.barcodeArSettings() with only EAN13_UPCA and QR enabled, BarcodeAr.forContext(...), a BarcodeArHighlightProvider returning a BarcodeArRectangleHighlight, a registerView(view: BarcodeArView) function that assigns the provider and calls start(), and start()/pause()/stop() lifecycle hooks. It also explains that BarcodeArView must be constructed by the Android/iOS host and passed into shared code, and shows the setup checklist.

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown and mentions adding com.scandit.datacapture.kmp:core and com.scandit.datacapture.kmp:barcode dependencies to commonMain
Setup checklist mentions adding CAMERA permission (AndroidManifest.xml) and NSCameraUsageDescription (Info.plist)
A license key placeholder string '-- ENTER YOUR SCANDIT LICENSE KEY HERE --' is present
DataCaptureContext.initialize( is used
BarcodeArSettings.barcodeArSettings() is used to construct settings (not a BarcodeArSettings() constructor)
Symbology.EAN13_UPCA is enabled
Symbology.QR is enabled
No symbologies other than EAN13_UPCA and QR are enabled
BarcodeAr.forContext( is used to construct the mode (not a BarcodeAr(...) direct constructor)
BarcodeArHighlightProvider is implemented
highlightForBarcode is overridden and returns a BarcodeArRectangleHighlight(barcode) constructed without a Context argument
The highlight callback is invoked as a function call (callback(highlight) or equivalent), not callback.onData(
A function that accepts an already-constructed BarcodeArView (e.g. registerView(view: BarcodeArView)) is present, since BarcodeArView cannot be constructed directly in shared code
view.highlightProvider is assigned
view.start() is called after the provider is assigned
start() and pause() are used to drive the view's lifecycle rather than onResume()/onPause()/onDestroy()

evals.prompt: Add an info annotation to each tracked barcode in my MatrixScan AR screen model. The annotation should show the barcode's data as a single body row. Here is my screen model: IntegratedScannerScreenModel.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill adds a BarcodeArAnnotationProvider implementation to the screen model, implements annotationForBarcode to return a BarcodeArInfoAnnotation with the barcode data as a body row, assigns it in registerView (view.annotationProvider), and preserves the existing highlight provider and lifecycle code.

assertions.text: BarcodeArAnnotationProvider is implemented
annotationForBarcode is overridden
BarcodeArInfoAnnotation(barcode) is constructed without a Context argument
The barcode data (barcode.data) is used as annotation body content via a BarcodeArInfoAnnotationBodyComponent
The annotation callback is invoked as a function call (callback(annotation) or equivalent), not callback.onData(
view.annotationProvider is assigned
Existing integration code (BarcodeAr, BarcodeArView registration, highlight provider) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to suppress the default beep and vibration in my MatrixScan AR KMP app — silent scanning. Update the BarcodeAr feedback.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets barcodeAr.feedback to a BarcodeArFeedback() (empty constructor, which produces silent feedback) and preserves the rest of the integration.

assertions.text: barcodeAr.feedback is reassigned to a BarcodeArFeedback() to suppress audio and haptic feedback
BarcodeArFeedback.defaultFeedback() is NOT used for this silent-scanning change
Existing integration code (BarcodeAr, BarcodeArView registration, highlight provider) is preserved

evals.prompt: Switch the highlight in my MatrixScan AR screen model from a rectangle to a pulsing circle highlight with a green brush. Here is my screen model: IntegratedScannerScreenModel.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill replaces the BarcodeArRectangleHighlight with a BarcodeArCircleHighlight, sets isPulsing to true, and customizes the brush with a green Color via Color.fromRgba, preserving the rest of the integration.

assertions.text: BarcodeArCircleHighlight(barcode, ...) is constructed with a BarcodeArCircleHighlightPreset
highlight.isPulsing is set to true
highlight.brush is assigned using Color.fromRgba( for a green color
BarcodeArRectangleHighlight is NOT used for this highlight in the updated code
Existing integration code (BarcodeAr, BarcodeArView registration, session listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Add a status icon annotation to my MatrixScan AR screen model that shows 'Expired' with a red exclamation-mark icon over each tracked barcode. Here is my screen model: IntegratedScannerScreenModel.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill implements BarcodeArAnnotationProvider returning a BarcodeArStatusIconAnnotation with text set to 'Expired' and an icon built via ScanditIcon.builder() with a red background and an exclamation-mark icon type, assigned to view.annotationProvider.

assertions.text: BarcodeArStatusIconAnnotation(barcode) is constructed
annotation.text is set to a string containing 'Expired'
annotation.icon is built with ScanditIcon.builder() using ScanditIconType.EXCLAMATION_MARK
view.annotationProvider is assigned
Existing integration code (BarcodeAr, BarcodeArView registration, highlight provider) is preserved

evals.prompt: Add a popover annotation to my MatrixScan AR screen model with Accept and Reject buttons that appears when the highlight is tapped. Log which button was tapped. Here is my screen model: IntegratedScannerScreenModel.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill implements BarcodeArAnnotationProvider returning a BarcodeArPopoverAnnotation constructed with two BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton instances (Accept, Reject), assigns a BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationListener that handles onPopoverButtonTapped, and wires the annotation provider into the view.

assertions.text: BarcodeArPopoverAnnotation(barcode, listOf(...)) is constructed with a list of BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton
A button labeled 'Accept' and a button labeled 'Reject' are created via BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton
BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationListener is implemented with onPopoverButtonTapped overridden
view.annotationProvider is assigned
Existing integration code (BarcodeAr, BarcodeArView registration, highlight provider) is preserved

evals.prompt: Show a small info annotation when a barcode is far away and a larger, more detailed one when it's close up, in my MatrixScan AR screen model. Here is my screen model: IntegratedScannerScreenModel.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill implements BarcodeArAnnotationProvider returning a BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation constructed from a close-up BarcodeArInfoAnnotation and a far-away BarcodeArInfoAnnotation, using the width presets to differentiate size.

assertions.text: BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation(barcode, closeUpAnnotation, farAwayAnnotation) is constructed from two BarcodeArInfoAnnotation instances
The close-up and far-away BarcodeArInfoAnnotation instances use different BarcodeArInfoAnnotationWidthPreset values
view.annotationProvider is assigned
Existing integration code (BarcodeAr, BarcodeArView registration, highlight provider) is preserved

evals.prompt: When the user taps a highlight in my MatrixScan AR screen model, I want to log the barcode data that was tapped. Here is my screen model: IntegratedScannerScreenModel.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill implements BarcodeArViewUiListener with onHighlightForBarcodeTapped(barcodeAr, barcode, highlight) (three parameters, no View parameter) and assigns it to view.uiListener in registerView.

assertions.text: BarcodeArViewUiListener is implemented
onHighlightForBarcodeTapped is overridden with exactly three parameters (barcodeAr, barcode, highlight) and no View/highlightView parameter
view.uiListener is assigned
Existing integration code (BarcodeAr, BarcodeArView registration, highlight provider) is preserved

evals.prompt: I only want barcodes starting with 'PROD-' to be augmented in my MatrixScan AR screen model — filter out everything else. Here is my screen model: IntegratedScannerScreenModel.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill implements BarcodeArFilter with filterBarcodes(barcodes) returning only barcodes whose data starts with 'PROD-', and registers it via barcodeAr.setBarcodeFilter(filter).

assertions.text: BarcodeArFilter is implemented
filterBarcodes(barcodes: List<Barcode>) is overridden and filters using barcode.data starting with 'PROD-'
barcodeAr.setBarcodeFilter( is called to register the filter
Existing integration code (BarcodeAr, BarcodeArView registration, highlight provider) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my KMP MatrixScan AR app, only show the info annotation after the user taps a highlight, not automatically.
evals.expected_output: Set the annotation's annotationTrigger to BarcodeArAnnotationTrigger.HIGHLIGHT_TAP.

assertions.text: annotationTrigger is set on the annotation (semantic)
BarcodeArAnnotationTrigger.HIGHLIGHT_TAP is present

evals.prompt: Show the torch and zoom controls on my KMP MatrixScan AR view.
evals.expected_output: Set shouldShowTorchControl and shouldShowZoomControl to true on the BarcodeArView.

assertions.text: shouldShowTorchControl is set to true on the BarcodeArView (semantic)
shouldShowZoomControl is set to true on the BarcodeArView (semantic)
